Summer Research Program

WordPress Research Fellows

A 10-week paid summer fellowship where undergraduate Software Engineering students build, test, and ship real WordPress tooling alongside faculty mentors.

Apply Now →

Program Overview

Build the tools you use every day

The WordPress Research Fellows Program pairs Software Engineering undergraduates with faculty mentors to solve real problems in plugin architecture, editor UX, performance, security, and accessibility — the same systems that power the department’s own web infrastructure.

Hands-on, mentored research

Each fellow is paired with a faculty mentor and joins a small track team focused on one problem area — plugin architecture, editor UX, performance, or accessibility. No prior WordPress experience required, just solid PHP or JS fundamentals.

  • Plugin Architecture
  • Editor & Block UX
  • Performance & Security
  • Accessibility
  • Developer Tooling
  • Cloud & Deployment

Ship something real

Fellows don’t just study code — every track ships a working plugin, block, or theme feature to a real test site by week 10, presented at the end-of-summer showcase.

Ready to spend your summer building?

Applications for Summer 2027 open December 1, 2026.

See Eligibility & Deadlines →
